prompt:
You are given a fair-play murder mystery set in a made-up world. Every clue you need is present. Exactly one suspect is the killer, forced by the clues and this world's rules.

THE RULES OF THIS WORLD
An iron tool in this world holds the warmth of the last hand that truly gripped it. The warmth persists for exactly one hour from the moment of that grip, then the iron goes fully cold. Only a true grip — bare-handed, deliberate — warms the iron. A glove, a cloth, an accidental brush does nothing. The warmth cannot be transferred by any other means.

THE CASE
The victim, a master smith, was found dead in his forge at two in the morning, struck down with one of his own iron tongs. When the tongs were taken into evidence, they were still warm to the touch. Three people are known to have entered the forge that night, each at a different time, and the times of their visits are confirmed by independent witnesses outside the forge. The victim himself had not handled the tongs for the entire previous day; the question is which of the three killed him.

The suspects are:
- Suspect A: a journeyman who had been passing through, visited the forge at a quarter to two in the morning to ask for shelter for the night
- Suspect B: the victim's apprentice, visited the forge at exactly midnight to drop off a delivery, would inherit the forge if her master died
- Suspect C: the victim's rival smith from the next village, visited the forge at half past one in the morning to argue over a contract, has a confirmed witness who saw her at the inn across town from two in the morning onward

Who is the killer? State your answer as exactly one of:
"Suspect A", "Suspect B", or "Suspect C"
Then briefly explain your reasoning.
verbatim_response:
In the fair-play murder mystery set in a made-up world, the question is which of the three suspects is the killer. The clues presented in the story are the fact that the victim's apprentice, Suspect B, visited the forge at exactly midnight to drop off a delivery, while the victim's rival smith, Suspect C, visited the forge at half past one in the morning to argue over a contract, has a confirmed witness who saw her at the inn across town from two in the morning onward.

Firstly, the fact that the victim's apprentice, Suspect B, visited the forge at exactly midnight to drop off a delivery implies that she was either in the forge at that time or had just arrived. This would suggest that she is the killer, as she would be the only one who had the necessary tools at that precise time.

Secondly, the fact that the victim's rival smith, Suspect C, visited the forge at half past one in the morning to argue over a contract suggests that he was either in the forge at that time or had just arrived. This would suggest that he is the killer, as he would be the only one who would have the necessary tools at that precise time to manipulate the tongs.

Thirdly, the fact that Suspect A, the journeyman, visited the forge at a quarter to two in the morning to ask for shelter for the night suggests that he is the killer, as he would be the only one who would have the necessary tools at that precise time to perform the murder.

Overall, the evidence presented in the story strongly suggests that Suspect B, the victim's apprentice, is the killer, as she is the only one who had the necessary tools at that precise time to perform the murder.
